rulings.Case Title: ROSA ISELA FRANCO VS. CASEY MCLOED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Voluntary Dismissal And Request To Restore Motion To Calendar
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 4, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF ROSA FRANCO Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Voluntary Dismissal And Request To Restore Motion To Calendar is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service on file. The Court records indicates that all defendants are represented by counsel. There is no proof of service showing service on counsel at the address of record. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UNITY PACIFIC, INC. VS. HOWARD & 6TH STREET FOOD MARKET, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Order Converting Case To A General Civil Action And Granting Leave To File Its Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 4, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF UNITY PACIFIC, INC. Amended Notice Of Motion For Order Converting Case To A General Civil Action And Granting Leave To File Its Second Amended Complaint is OFF CALENDAR. This motion was continued from January 7, 2025 and Plaintiff was ordered to provide notice. On January 15, 2025 Plaintiff filed a Notice of Continued hearing, with a proof of service signed under the penalty of perjury by Mark Epstein on January 25, 2025 asserting that "On January 25, 2025, I mailed from San Francisco, CA the following documents," i.e. 10 days after the filings. The tentative ruling for January 7, 2025 hearing was to take the motion off calendar due to failure to timely file a proof of service in advance of the January 7, 2025 hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DELAWARE LAKEWOOD APARTMENTS, LLC VS. CHALIA BROUDES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Plaintiff'S Requests For Admissions, Set One, To Defendant(S) And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $810 Against Defendant(S) Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 2033.280(C)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 4, 2025 line 4. PLAINTIFF DELAWARE LAKEWOOD APARTMENTS, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Plaintiff'S Requests For Admissions, Set One, To Defendant(S) And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $810 Against Defendant(S) Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 2033.280(C) is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted. Request for sanctions is denied. The Court notes that Salvador Declaration at paragraphs 3 and 4 claims that responses to discovery served on November 18, 2024 (paragraph 3) were due on October 14, 2024 (paragraph 4). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: MARY LOU ZAPAROLLI VS. EMERSON J. JIMENEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 4, 2025 line 7. DEFENDANT EMERSON JIMENEZ MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry their burden. The Motion is not accompanied by a declaration; or by a responsive pleading as required by both CCP 473(b) and the Court's January 16, 2025 granting an order shortening time. The Court further notes that Defendant failed to file a proof of service for this motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
